q1.prompt: A 60-year-old woman is admitted with extreme fatigue, a 3-kg weight gain in one month, and orthopnoea. Clinical examination reveals cyanosis, distended neck veins, and a ventricular ejection fraction of 0.30. Which of the following best describes the multi-system compensation occurring in this patient?

options.label: Increased ANP secretion leading to a massive increase in glomerular filtration rate to reduce ECF volume.
Increased sympathetic discharge and activation of the renin-angiotensin-aldosterone system to maintain arterial pressure despite reduced cardiac output.
A leftward shift of the Frank-Starling curve to increase stroke volume at any given end-diastolic volume.
Decreased pulmonary capillary hydrostatic pressure to prevent further transudation of fluid into the alveoli.
A decrease in wall stress according to Laplace's Law due to the compensatory dilation of the left ventricle.

q2.prompt: A mountaineer who has been at a high-altitude base camp for four days exhibits a calculated arterial pH of 7.42, a Pa_(CO2)of 28 mmHg, and a decreased plasma [HCO3-]. Which of the following mechanisms is primarily responsible for the stabilisation of her arterial pH?

options.label: Peripheral chemoreceptor adaptation to low arterial P_(O2)levels.
Decreased synthesis of 2,3-bisphosphoglycerate in red blood cells to improve oxygen loading in the lungs.
Massive renal secretion of H^+to buffer the alkaline load in the cerebrospinal fluid.
Renal excretion of HCO3-  to compensate for the respiratory alkalosis induced by hypoxia-driven hyperventilation.
Shunting of blood away from the brain to prevent hypocapnia-induced cerebral vasodilation.

q3.prompt: A 65-year-old patient with uncontrolled Type I diabetes mellitus presents in a coma with a blood glucose of 500 mg/dL and a plasma pH of 7.12. Laboratory analysis reveals a high anion gap. How would you analyze the integration of the respiratory and renal systems in this state?

options.label: The kidneys respond to the ketoacidosis by increasing HCO3-excretion to lower the plasma pH further.
The peripheral chemoreceptors sense the decrease in arterial [H^+] and inhibit the medullary inspiratory center.
The brain has switched to using glucose exclusively to protect against the toxic effects of ketone bodies.
Alveolar hyperventilation occurs as a compensatory mechanism to lower Pa_(CO2)  and mitigate the decrease in arterial pH.
A mixed acid-base disorder is present where respiratory acidosis prevents the renal generation of new bicarbonate.

q4.prompt: A patient is brought to the emergency department following a motor vehicle accident with a ruptured spleen and a blood pressure of 80/50 mmHg. Which of the following integrated cardiovascular and endocrine responses would be observed in the first 30 minutes?

options.label: Decreased firing rate of the carotid sinus nerve and a resulting increase in sympathetic outflow to the heart and systemic arterioles.
Increased firing rate of the arterial baroreceptors leading to widespread vasodilation to improve tissue perfusion.
Inhibition of the renin-angiotensin-aldosterone system to prevent hypertensive damage to the kidneys.
A decrease in heart rate mediated by the Bainbridge reflex in response to reduced atrial filling.
Activation of the parasympathetic nervous system to decrease myocardial O2  consumption.

q5.prompt: During a strenuous aerobic exercise test, an athlete's cardiac output increases five-fold, while her mean arterial pressure increases only slightly. Which statement best analyzes the integration of the vascular and metabolic responses that allow for this outcome?

options.label: The baroreceptor reflex is suppressed by the motor cortex to allow for a simultaneous drop in heart rate and blood pressure.
Total peripheral resistance increases dramatically to ensure that blood flow is directed exclusively to the brain and heart.
Total peripheral resistance decreases because local metabolic vasodilation in active skeletal muscle overrides the sympathetic vasoconstriction in non-essential organs.
The respiratory pump decreases venous return, thereby preventing the heart from exceeding its maximum stroke volume.
Renal blood flow increases significantly to facilitate the excretion of lactic acid and heat.

q6.prompt: A 24-year-old woman presents with severe dehydration and hypotension following five days of persistent diarrhea. Which of the following sets of metabolic and cardiovascular abnormalities is most likely present?

options.label: Hyperkalemia, metabolic alkalosis, and a high anion gap.
Hypokalemia, metabolic acidosis with a normal anion gap, and orthostatic hypotension.
Hypernatremia, respiratory acidosis, and bradycardia.
Hypochloremia, metabolic alkalosis, and an increased effective circulating volume.
Normal serum electrolytes, metabolic acidosis, and an increased firing rate of the carotid sinus nerves.

q7.prompt: A patient suffers a significant head injury that results in a rapid increase in intracranial pressure (ICP). Which of the following describes the Cushing reaction and the integrated response of the vasomotor centers?

options.label: Decreased mean arterial pressure and tachycardia to prevent cerebral edema.
Massive parasympathetic discharge leading to hypotension and airway constriction.
Inhibition of the peripheral chemoreceptors by high levels of cerebrospinal fluid O2 .
Systemic hypotension and reflex tachycardia to reduce the workload of the heart.
Systemic hypertension and reflex bradycardia in response to cerebral ischemia.

q8.prompt: A 72-year-old man with a lung tumor is found to have a plasma Na^+ concentration of 113 mEq/L and a plasma osmolarity of 230 mOsm/L. He suffers a grand mal seizure. What is the analyze of the underlying pathophysiology and the most appropriate integrative intervention?

options.label: Ectopic ADH secretion has caused excessive water reabsorption and brain cell swelling; treat with water restriction.
Primary polydipsia has caused hypernatremia and neuronal dehydration; treat with a large bolus of pure water.
Adrenal insufficiency has led to excessive sodium loss in the urine; treat with high doses of aldosterone.
Diabetes insipidus has caused a massive loss of free water; treat with a V2 receptor agonist.
The tumor has destroyed the thirst center in the hypothalamus; treat with forced oral fluid intake.

q9.prompt: A woman in her third trimester of pregnancy is diagnosed with preeclampsia, characterized by a rapid rise in blood pressure and proteinuria. Which of the following is an integrated explanation for this clinical pathophysiology?

options.label: Excessive consumption of dietary salt leading to a primary volume-loading hypertension.
A decrease in the production of angiotensin II due to the auto-immune destruction of the lungs.
Placental ischemia releasing anti-angiogenic factors into the maternal circulation that impair endothelial function and trigger hypertension.
A massive decrease in systemic vascular resistance that causes the heart to fail as a pump.
The suppression of the fetal hypothalamic-pituitary-adrenal axis, which prevents the maturation of maternal blood vessels.

q10.prompt: A subject participating in a prolonged hunger strike reaches Day 10 of total fasting. Which of the following adaptions has occurred to maintain homeostasis and prevent the loss of consciousness?

options.label: The brain has stopped using oxygen and switched entirely to anaerobic glycolysis using muscle glycogen.
The liver has converted all stored triglycerides into glucose to keep the plasma glucose above 180 mg/dL.
Insulin levels have increased ten-fold to facilitate the storage of any remaining amino acids into the skeletal muscle.
The brain has adapted to use ketone bodies synthesized by the liver from fatty acids as its primary fuel source.
Peripheral chemoreceptors have been inhibited to reduce the metabolic work of breathing by 90%.
